pi_items.source: Task description: 'carrying 24 amperes of line current'

Calculated using standard three-phase power formula: S = √3 × V_L × I_L = 1.732051 × 400 × 24 = 16627.69 VA. Metadata value is correctly rounded to nearest integer. Verified via web search of three-phase power calculation formulas (sources: electricityforum.com, omnicalculator.com, electricalpereview.com)

Calculated using real power formula: P = S × PF = 16627.69 × 0.80 = 13302.15 W. Metadata value is correctly rounded to nearest integer. Consistent with verifier.py hardcoded value of 13301.8 (within 0.001% error)

Calculated using reactive power formula: Q = S × sin(arccos(PF)) = 16627.69 × sin(arccos(0.80)) = 16627.69 × 0.60 = 9976.61 VAR. Metadata value is correctly rounded to nearest integer. Verified via power factor calculator resources and confirmed through 3-4-5 right triangle relationship where sin(arccos(0.80)) = 0.60 exactly
Confirmed in README.md stating '±3%' tolerance and verifier.py showing TOLERANCE_PCT = 3.0. All expected power values fall within ±3% of calculated values

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies a 3-phase, 400V (line-to-line), 24A distribution feeder with 0.80 lagging power factor. Using the standard three-phase power calculation formulas (S = √3 × V × I; P = S × PF; Q = S × sin(arccos(PF))), the expected power values are: Apparent Power = 16627.69 VA, Real Power = 13302.15 W, Reactive Power = 9976.61 VAR. The metadata claims (16627 VA, 13302 W, 9976 VAR) are correctly rounded integers of these calculated values. All calculations verified against authoritative electrical engineering formulas and references. The tolerance is ±3%. Data is synthetic (calculated from input parameters) but mathematically correct.
